sections.heading: TNM
sections.content: {"system": "CNS tumors do not use AJCC TNM staging. Gliomas, ependymomas, medulloblastoma, and meningiomas are classified by WHO histologic and molecular grade (2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the CNS). Brain metastases are classified as 'limited' versus 'extensive' based on treatability with SRS versus WBRT. Leptomeningeal metastases are stratified by risk (good vs. poor) rather than anatomic stage. Metastatic spine tumors are classified by anatomic location (extradural, intradural-extramedullary, intradural-intramedullary) and presence of spinal cord compression.", "t_stages": [{"stage": "Not applicable", "description": "CNS primary tumors do not use T staging. Tumors are classified by WHO histologic and molecular grade (Grade 1-4) and integrated molecular diagnosis per WHO 2021 classification."}], "n_stages": [{"stage": "Not applicable", "description": "CNS primary tumors do not use N staging. Nodal disease is not applicable to primary brain tumors. For brain metastases, extent of disease is classified as limited vs. extensive."}], "m_stages": [{"stage": "Not applicable for primary CNS tumors", "description": "Primary CNS tumors are staged by WHO grade, not M stage. Medulloblastoma uses modified Chang staging (M0 = no metastasis, M1 = CSF cytology positive, M2 = intracranial metastases beyond primary, M3 = spinal drop metastases, M4 = extraneural metastases). Leptomeningeal metastases represent disseminated disease within the CNS."}], "stage_groups": [{"group": "WHO Grade 1 — Circumscribed Gliomas", "criteria": "Pilocytic astrocytoma, subependymal giant cell astrocytoma (SEGA), ganglioglioma, dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumor (DNET). Noninvasive, surgically curable [GLIO-1, MS-7-8]", "clinical_meaning": "Generally indolent, curable with surgery alone. SEGA associated with tuberous sclerosis complex.", "five_yr_survival": "Excellent (>90% for pilocytic astrocytoma)", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "WHO Grade 2 — Oligodendroglioma (IDH-mutant, 1p19q codeleted)", "criteria": "IDH1/2-mutant with 1p/19q whole-arm codeletion. Non-enhancing or minimally enhancing on MRI [GLIO-2, MS-9]", "clinical_meaning": "Relatively favorable prognosis among diffuse gliomas. Responds to RT and alkylating chemotherapy. 1p/19q codeletion and IDH mutation both confer favorable prognosis [MS-8, MS-13-14]", "five_yr_survival": "~80%-90% with combined modality treatment", "treatment_intent": "Potentially long-term disease control; observation possible after GTR"}, {"group": "WHO Grade 2 — IDH-mutant Astrocytoma", "criteria": "IDH1/2-mutant without 1p/19q codeletion. No ATRX loss excluded. No CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion [GLIO-4, MS-9-10]", "clinical_meaning": "Intermediated prognosis between oligodendroglioma and glioblastoma. Progresses faster than grade 2 oligodendroglioma. IDH mutation confers favorable prognosis [MS-8-9]", "five_yr_survival": "~70%-80%", "treatment_intent": "Long-term disease control; observation possible after GTR in select cases"}, {"group": "WHO Grade 3 — Oligodendroglioma (IDH-mutant, 1p19q codeleted)", "criteria": "Higher-grade oligodendroglioma with IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion [GLIO-3, MS-14-15]", "clinical_meaning": "Better prognosis than other grade 3-4 gliomas. Significant survival benefit from RT + PCV (EORTC 26951: OS not reached in RT+PCV vs 112 months RT alone in 1p19q codeleted subgroup) [MS-161-162]. RTOG 9402: doubling of median OS with PCV in 1p19q codeleted tumors (14.7 vs 7.3 years) [MS-163-164]", "five_yr_survival": "~70%-80% with RT + PCV", "treatment_intent": "Long-term disease control with RT + PCV"}, {"group": "WHO Grade 3 — IDH-mutant Astrocytoma", "criteria": "IDH1/2-mutant astrocytoma, grade 3 by histology [GLIO-5, MS-15-16]", "clinical_meaning": "CATNON trial showed benefit of adjuvant TMZ in IDH-mutant anaplastic gliomas (HR 0.48, P < .0001) [MS-167]. RT + adjuvant TMZ is preferred.", "five_yr_survival": "~56% with RT + TMZ (CATNON 5-year data)", "treatment_intent": "Long-term disease control"}, {"group": "WHO Grade 4 — IDH-mutant Astrocytoma", "criteria": "IDH1/2-mutant astrocytoma with necrosis/microvascular proliferation OR CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion, regardless of classic histologic features [GLIO-6, MS-6, MS-23]", "clinical_meaning": "Prognosis better than IDH wild-type glioblastoma. CATNON trial benefit from adjuvant TMZ extrapolated. RT + concurrent and adjuvant TMZ is preferred [MS-140, MS-167]", "five_yr_survival": "~40%-50% with RT + TMZ", "treatment_intent": "Long-term disease control"}, {"group": "Glioblastoma, IDH1/2 Wild-Type (WHO Grade 4)", "criteria": "Diffusely infiltrative astrocytoma with IDH wild-type status AND at least one of: necrosis, microvascular proliferation, EGFR amplification, gain of chr7/loss of chr10, or TERT promoter mutation [GLIO-10-11, MS-6, MS-22]", "clinical_meaning": "Most common malignant brain tumor in adults. Very poor prognosis. Median OS 14.6 months with RT + TMZ (EORTC-NCIC) [MS-160]. MGMT promoter methylati"} ]}

t_stages.description: CNS primary tumors do not use T staging. Tumors are classified by WHO histologic and molecular grade (Grade 1-4) and integrated molecular diagnosis per WHO 2021 classification.

n_stages.description: CNS primary tumors do not use N staging. Nodal disease is not applicable to primary brain tumors. For brain metastases, extent of disease is classified as limited vs. extensive.

m_stages.category: Not applicable for primary CNS tumors
m_stages.description: Primary CNS tumors are staged by WHO grade, not M stage. Medulloblastoma uses modified Chang staging (M0 = no metastasis, M1 = CSF cytology positive, M2 = intracranial metastases beyond primary, M3 = spinal drop metastases, M4 = extraneural metastases). Leptomeningeal metastases represent disseminated disease within the CNS.
